zh.Chatsworth House, Bakewell, Derbyshire,: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾哈斯沃斯

zh.lifeSummary: 當John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk於1270年出生在英國德比夏爾時，他的父親Edmund Le Leche40歲，母親Mrs. Margaret Le Leche25歲。他於1293年之前與Lucy Cawarden結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1370年在英國爾享年100歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk于1270年出生在英国德比夏爾时，他的父亲Edmund Le Leche40岁，母亲Mrs. Margaret Le Leche25岁。他于1293年之前与Lucy Cawarden结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1370年在英国爾享年100岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Leche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of   Letch   or   Leech  .

variants.name: Letch
meanings: English: locative name, either topographic from Middle English lech(e), lecche, lache ‘sluggish stream, wet place’ (Old English * læcc(e), * lecc(e) ‘stream’), or toponymic from a place named with this word, such as Lach Dennis and Lache (both Cheshire) and Eastleach and Northleach (both Gloucs).

variants.name: Leech
meanings: Irish (Galway):English name adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó MaolMhaodhóg (see Logue ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk was born in 1270, in Derbyshire, England, his father, Edmund Le Leche, was 40 and his mother, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, was 25. He married Lucy Cawarden before 1293. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1370, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 100.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk est né en 1270, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Le Leche, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Lucy Cawarden avant 1293. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1370, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 100 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk 1270 in Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund Le Leche 40 und seine Mutter Mrs. Margaret Le Leche 25. Er hat vor 1293 Lucy Cawarden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1370 im Alter von 100 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk è nato il nel 1270 nel Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, Edmund Le Leche, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Lucy Cawarden prima del 1293. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1370, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 100 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolkが1270年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Edmund Le Lecheは40歳，母Mrs. Margaret Le Lecheは25歳であった。1293年以前にLucy Cawardenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1370年にイギリスチェシュアイアで100歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk(이)가 1270년 영국 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Le Leche(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Margaret Le Leche(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1293년 이전 Lucy Cawarden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1370년 영국 체실르에서 100세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk nasceu em 1270, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Le Leche, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy Cawarden antes 1293. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1370, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 100 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk родился в 1270 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Le Leche было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Cawarden перед 1293 г. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1370 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 100 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk nació en 1270, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Le Leche, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Lucy Cawarden antes de 1293. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1370, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 100 años.
